nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: White-browed tit-spinetail
nodes.summary: White-browed tit-spinetail（White-browed tit-spinetail，学名 Leptasthenura xenothorax），在图谱中标记为近危（NT）物种，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It primarily inhabits Polylepis woodlands but occasionally occurs in montane scrublands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Primary threats to the species include habitat degradation from fires and grazing. "High-Andean Polylepis forest ecosystems represent one of the most threatened woodland ecosystems in the world...which continues to decline in quantity and quality." On the brighter side, "[c]ommunity-based woodland habitat restoration efforts are currently underway at three locations in the Cordillera Vilcanota (Abra Malaga, Hulloc and Cancha-Cancha) under the guidance of the Peruvian non-governmental organization Asociación Ecosistemas Andinos (ECOAN)."
